hero.name: Egor Polyakov
hero.subtitle: AI is very good at how. Architecture is about why.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Solution Architect. I ask why before asking how.

whoIAm.title: Who I Am
whoIAm.p1: Graduated from LNMO in St. Petersburg, explored over a dozen programming languages, and placed in ICYS and Baltic Science and Engineering Competition.
whoIAm.p2: From dynamic EAV systems to cloud-native Go microservices to Imperia OS — a platform where AI is part of the architecture, not bolted on.

approach.title: How I Think
approach.p1: I ask why before asking how. Most engineers optimize the solution. I check whether the problem is stated correctly first.
approach.p2: Strong at catching when a concept drifts mid-reasoning — when a generic label hides the fact that nothing concrete was built, or when an abstraction solves a problem that doesn't exist.

currentFocus.title: Current Focus
currentFocus.p1: Imperia OS — the same infrastructure builds the platform and delivers it to its users.

continuousLearning.title: Continuous Learning
continuousLearning.text: Every project provides new thinking tools.

curiosity.title: Curiosity
curiosity.text: Seeking root causes, not just treating symptoms.

resume.title: Resume

imperiaOs.title: Imperia OS
imperiaOs.subtitle: AI-first development platform.

problem.title: The Problem
problem.text: Most AI integrations are stateless — no memory between sessions, no model of the business, no ability to act on real systems. Each interaction starts from zero.

solution.title: Architecture
solution.text: State-machine runtime where LLMs are atomic steps — the algorithm controls the flow, not the model. Knowledge graphs give agents business context. Infrastructure deploys and monitors itself.

tasks: State-machine orchestration — every LLM call is a deterministic, auditable step
Knowledge graphs: business domain, test scenarios, code structure — each with its own schema
MCP tool system — agents access PostgreSQL, Kuzu, MongoDB through typed interfaces
Thought engine — persistent memory that connects ideas across sessions
Hot standby with automatic failover and zero-downtime deployment

howWeWork.title: Development Flow
howWeWork.note: Same infrastructure builds the platform and delivers it.

steps: Business scenarios defined in the knowledge graph
Agents read the graph, write code, deploy — same pipeline for everything

whyNotConsulting.title: Why This Approach
whyNotConsulting.problem: Typical AI integrations bolt an LLM onto an existing system. No context, no memory. Every call is a cold start.
whyNotConsulting.solution: Imperia OS makes LLMs stateful. The algorithm doesn't hallucinate — it controls the flow, and each step is atomic. Memory persists across sessions. Knowledge graphs provide business context.

caseStudies.title: Case Studies
caseStudies.subtitle: Deep dives into technical solutions and architectural decisions

imperia-os.title: Imperia OS — AI-First Development Platform
imperia-os.description: AI-first platform where the algorithm controls the flow and LLMs are atomic steps.
imperia-os.problem: AI assistants are stateless — no persistent memory, no business context, no ability to act on real systems. Building a real assistant requires a platform: runtime, knowledge base, agent system, and infrastructure.
imperia-os.solution: State-machine runtime where each LLM call is a bounded step in an algorithm. Knowledge graphs for business domain, tests, and code — each with its own schema. MCP tool system for typed database access. Hot-standby infrastructure with automatic failover.
technicalDetails: State-machine runtime — the algorithm doesn't hallucinate
Knowledge graphs in Kuzu: business domain, tests, code structure
MCP tool system — PostgreSQL, Kuzu, MongoDB via typed interfaces
Thought engine — persistent memory with wikilinks and embeddings
Hot standby with 15s health checks and automatic failover
results: The same infrastructure builds the platform and delivers it to users
Development flow: business scenarios → tests → agents write code
lessonsLearned: LLMs become reliable when each call is a bounded step in a state machine
Separate knowledge graphs beat one merged graph — different query patterns need different schemas
Building the platform with itself is the strongest validation

mobile-platform.title: Cross-Platform Mobile Platform
mobile-platform.description: Scalable mobile platform on Flutter and AWS with clean architecture and automated infrastructure.
mobile-platform.problem: Needed a foundation for a cross-platform product with reliable backend and automated deployment. Typical approaches led to fragmented architecture.
mobile-platform.solution: Implemented Clean Architecture with Feature-based structure in Flutter. Backend - serverless on AWS (Lambda in Golang). Infrastructure defined in CDK and deploys automatically.
technicalDetails: Serverless backend on AWS (Lambda, DynamoDB, Cognito)
lessonsLearned: Clean Architecture pays off on large projects
Infrastructure as Code is critical for reproducibility

website-builder.title: Dynamic Website Builder Platform
website-builder.description: Flexible website builder system with dynamic data schema and multi-tenancy support.
website-builder.problem: Needed a builder where users can create arbitrary entity types and fields without DB migrations. Plus data isolation between clients.
website-builder.solution: Implemented EAV (Entity-Attribute-Value) pattern with multi-tenancy at the database level. Users create their entities and fields through the interface, data is stored in a universal and extensible structure.
technicalDetails: EAV pattern for dynamic schema without migrations
Multi-tenancy with isolated databases per client
Type-safe storage of different data types
results: Users create entities without developer involvement
System adapts to different business domains
lessonsLearned: EAV provides maximum flexibility but requires query optimization
Multi-tenancy at DB level is the most reliable isolation
